$schema_purpose: Public contract of theme-aware classes and CSS variables this app ships. Names are stable; values change when the user re-themes. Use this file when authoring Vue components in this repo or any downstream Frappe app.
$naming_convention: All site-wide emitted classes are namespaced with a 'theme-' prefix (e.g. theme-bg-primary, theme-text-muted, theme-rounded). The prefix prevents collisions with Frappe Desk / Bootstrap / vanilla Tailwind utility names (bg-primary, text-muted, border, …) whose cascade would otherwise override the theme on Desk pages. CSS variables are unchanged (--nce-color-*). The prefix applies to the emitted nce_theme.css consumed by downstream Frappe apps on Desk; the editor SPA's own internal Tailwind build is separate.

source_of_truth.tailwind_config: frontend/tailwind.config.js (editor SPA internal build only — not the downstream/Desk class names)

palette_scoping.summary: Utility classes are theme-agnostic — they paint with whatever --nce-* variables are in scope.
palette_scoping.default: :root holds the Site Theme Config.base_theme palette (the site fallback).
palette_scoping.scoped_palettes: Each NCE Theme with status=Active also gets [data-nce-theme="<slug>"] { --nce-* … } in nce_theme.css.
palette_scoping.consumer_usage: Set data-nce-theme="<slug>" on an ancestor element to scope a subtree to that palette. Unknown or empty slug inherits :root.
palette_scoping.custom_css: custom_css is global and emitted only on :root — not inside scoped palette blocks.
palette_scoping.class_layer: The theme-* utility class layer is emitted once; no class changes are needed for per-element theming.

status_legend.shipping: Class is emitted by css_writer.py into nce_theme.css today — safe to use immediately on Desk after republish.
status_legend.var-only: CSS variable is emitted but no class maps to it. Use :style="{ ... }" with var() until a class is added.
status_legend.proposed: Not implemented yet. Do not use in production components — included so authors know the planned API.

usage_rules: Prefer the theme-prefixed class names over raw CSS var() — class names are the public API.
Use var() inline only when the token name is dynamic (e.g. looping over palette roles).
Never hardcode hex literals in components. The editor → publish → var() → class chain is broken the moment you do.
For foreground/background pairing, prefer the paired class form (e.g. theme-bg-secondary theme-text-secondary-fg) over hand-picking text-white/text-black.
Do not add the theme- prefix to vanilla layout utilities (flex, items-center, border-b, font-bold) — those are not emitted by this theme and keep their normal names.
Buttons on panels: read button_hierarchy — theme-bg-primary for Submit/Apply; theme-bg-primary-100 theme-border for Cancel/Revert; never frappe outline with --nce-color-bg fill.

primary.purpose: Main brand color. Default action buttons, primary links, key UI accents.

foreground_mono.name: theme-text-primary-fg
foreground_mono.description: Black or white, whichever has best contrast against theme-bg-primary.

foreground_tonal.name: theme-text-primary-fg-tonal
foreground_tonal.description: Same hue as primary, lightness flipped, chroma reduced.

shade_scale.note: All 11 shade stops have emitted classes.

secondary.purpose: Supporting brand color. Secondary buttons, headers, alternate emphasis.

shade_scale.note: All 11 shade stops have emitted classes.

accent.purpose: Highlight color for emphasis, callouts, decorative accents.

shade_scale.note: All 11 shade stops have emitted classes.

success.purpose: Positive state — confirmations, healthy metrics, success badges.

shade_scale.note: All 11 shade stops have emitted classes.

info.purpose: Informational state — neutral notifications, help, tips.

shade_scale.note: All 11 shade stops have emitted classes.

warning.purpose: Caution state — warnings, pending items, attention needed.

shade_scale.note: All 11 shade stops have emitted classes.

danger.purpose: Error/destructive state — failures, delete buttons, critical alerts.

shade_scale.note: All 11 shade stops have emitted classes.

surface.purpose: Card/panel/modal background — one elevation above the page.

card_alias.purpose: Alias of theme-bg-surface for semantic clarity in card components.

header_bg.purpose: Top-bar/header strip background (paired with primary foreground).

themed_bg.purpose: Single class accepting a runtime --bg CSS variable. Foreground is computed live via OKLCH relative color and follows whatever --bg resolves to.

default_body.purpose: Body copy. Applies via the :root var; rarely set explicitly.

muted.purpose: Secondary copy — captions, helper text, timestamps.

default_border.purpose: Card edges, dividers, input borders (1px solid).

button_hierarchy.purpose: Filled shade buttons with auto mono fg — do not use frappe-ui variant=outline or page-background fill on controls that sit on panels.

main_cta.notes: Submit, Apply, Perform Find. theme-bg-primary sets paired -fg automatically.

quiet_secondary.notes: Cancel, Revert, Save, secondary toolbar actions on theme-bg-surface panels. Uses shade 100 + mono fg from emit pairing — not --nce-color-bg (page).

emphasis_secondary.notes: Second filled action when it must read stronger than 100 but weaker than base primary (e.g. active tab, Go).

button_hierarchy.panels: Modals/cards use theme-bg-surface or theme-bg-card (--nce-color-surface). Page only on theme-bg-bg-page / body chrome.

things_not_to_do: Don't write hex literals (#3b82f6) in components.
Don't write color: white as a default on a class that may sit on a light background.
Don't duplicate semantic colors inside <style scoped> when a theme- utility exists — layout-only scoped CSS is fine.
Don't drop the theme- prefix on Desk/downstream pages — bare names collide with Bootstrap.
Don't use frappe-ui Button variant=outline (or scoped CSS) with background var(--nce-color-bg) for buttons on panels — page bg is not control fill.
Don't map --bg-card or card/modal fills to --nce-color-bg; use --nce-color-surface (theme-bg-surface / theme-bg-card).
Don't use theme-bg-primary-500 for Cancel/Revert next to theme-bg-primary Submit — use theme-bg-primary-100 for quiet actions (see button_hierarchy).
